pattern: A segment tree over the value domain answers the windowed maximum query for each element, turning the bounded-gap longest-increasing-subsequence into an O(n log V) sweep.
hints: Define dp[v] as the best subsequence length ending exactly at value v; for the current value you may extend any prior value in a bounded range below it.
The valid predecessors for value x lie in the window [x-k, x-1], so you need the maximum dp over a value range.
Index dp by value and use a max segment tree: for each x query the max on [x-k, x-1], set dp[x] = that + 1, and track the global maximum.

edge_inputs.description: bounded gaps trim a longer run
moderate gap allows a length-four chain
two values with gap larger than k stay length one
strictly increasing means equal values cannot chain
consecutive values with k one form a long chain

stress.description: random values and gap over a moderate domain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 2000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 200)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: domain kept moderate so the brute-force oracle stays fast
